missions.name: Bill of Landing
missions.description: Nobody trades in the Expanse without paperwork. Customs Officer Larsen at Nexus Prime can issue a bill of landing, assuming you can cover the filing fee and don't mind the way she looks at your ship.
missions.hint: Speak with Customs Officer Larsen at the Nexus Prime cantina to get your trade permit.

timeliness_comments.timely: Intake clerk checks the count, nods once. "On the clock. Clean haul. Pay's good."
timeliness_comments.late: Intake clerk thumbs through the docket, doesn't look up. "Ran long. Pay's docked, not blocked. Don't make a habit of it."
timeliness_comments.very_late: Intake clerk leans on the crate and sighs. "Figured you'd forgotten. Had to source the last of it from the scrapyards. This is what's left of your pay."

missions.name: Iron Ore Delivery
missions.description: A cargo broker at Nexus Prime needs ten units of iron ore moved to Forgeworks. Straightforward hauling work, the kind that keeps your fuel tank full and your name on the reliable list. Every trader in the Expanse started with a run like this.
missions.hint: Talk to the cargo broker at Nexus Prime, then fly the iron ore to the intake clerk at the Neutral Ground on Forgeworks.
missions.discovery_text: Overheard a cargo broker near the docks looking for someone to haul iron ore to Forgeworks. Sounded like steady work for a new permit holder.

objectives.description: Deliver the ore to the intake clerk at Forgeworks

missions.name: Footing the Bill
missions.description: A woman named Elena Reeves flagged you down at the Nexus Prime cantina. Former Guild navigator, sharp eyes, sharper tongue. She offered trading advice like she was doing you a favor, and she probably was. But nobody gives something for nothing out here.
missions.hint: Elena Reeves will appear at Nexus Prime after completing the iron delivery. Listen to her trading advice.

missions.name: Union Territory
missions.description: Hanna Voss runs the Breakstone docks with a wrench in one hand and a grudge against the Commerce Guild in the other. She'll let you trade here, but only after you prove you're not another profiteer. Five units of food for the miners' commissary. Fair price for trust.
missions.hint: Buy 5 food and deliver it to Breakstone to earn the miners' trust. Hanna Voss is waiting.

missions.name: The Foreman's Son
missions.description: Marcus Jin went quiet when he heard your name. The Breakstone engineer knew your father. Or at least, he knew about the safety report that could have saved him. The one that got buried. He's carrying something, and it's not just guilt.
missions.hint: Talk to Marcus Jin at Breakstone. He knows something about your father.

objectives.description: Learn what Marcus knows about your father

missions.name: The Scholar's Errand
missions.description: Dr. Priya Osei's chartered transport was quietly reassigned. She suspects political pressure, though she's too polite to say it outright. Her research samples degrade by the hour, and Axiom Labs is the only facility that can process them. She needs a ride. You need a reason to visit Science Collective space.
missions.hint: Transport Dr. Priya Osei to Axiom Labs. She's waiting at Nexus Prime.

objectives.description: Transport Dr. Osei to Axiom Labs

missions.name: The Drifter's Deal
missions.description: Tomas Drifter has a smile that could sell atmo to a fish. He cornered you at Haven's Rest with a proposal involving Alliance textiles, Guild tariff loopholes, and what he calls 'creative commerce.' The profit margins are real. The legality is... interpretive.
missions.hint: Meet Tomas Drifter at Haven's Rest. He has a proposition for you.

missions.name: Drifter's Delivery
missions.description: Five bolts of Alliance textiles, your bill of landing, and Stellaris Port's premium buyers. Tomas can't sell there himself. He's got the wrong paperwork, the wrong reputation, and the wrong answers for customs. You're the clean hands in this arrangement. Sell the goods, split the take, and try not to think too hard about the tariff codes.
missions.hint: Travel to Stellaris Port and sell Tomas's textiles at the market.

missions.name: Recruit Navigator
missions.description: Elena's been stuck at Nexus Prime running freight projections for ships that aren't hers. She's too good for dock work and she knows it. Return to Nexus Prime and offer her a berth before someone else does.
missions.hint: Return to Nexus Prime to recruit Elena Reeves as your navigator.

missions.name: Recruit Engineer
missions.description: Marcus keeps that engine bay at Breakstone running on spit and stubbornness, but the shipyard has nothing left to teach him. He's ready to leave. He just needs someone to ask. Return to Breakstone before the mines swallow another good mechanic.
missions.hint: Return to Breakstone to recruit Marcus Jin as your engineer.

missions.name: Recruit Scientist
missions.description: Priya's lab at Axiom is pristine, well-funded, and slowly killing her curiosity. She needs field data, not simulations. A ship with cargo space and a captain who doesn't flinch at uncharted readings is worth more to her than tenure.
missions.hint: Travel to Axiom Labs to recruit Dr. Priya Osei as your scientist.

missions.name: Recruit Trader
missions.description: Tomas has been working solo since his family's business collapsed. He's good at reading people, better at reading markets, and tired of sleeping in transit berths. He proposed a partnership at Haven's Rest. His contacts, your ship. Worst case, you get a decent card player.
missions.hint: Travel to Haven's Rest to recruit Tomas Drifter as your trader.

missions.name: Cargo Lost
missions.description: The last three Guild convoys to Nova Research never arrived. Electronics shipments, vanished between jump points. The station's running on backup systems and borrowed time. Someone has to make the run, and someone has to find out why the pirates always know the route.
missions.hint: Deliver 5 electronics to Nova Research. Beware of pirates on the route.

objectives.description: Speak with Captain Sato at Nova Research

missions.name: Whispers at the Bar
missions.description: Dex Halloran found you at the Nexus Prime cantina. Not the other way around. He bought a drink he didn't touch and started talking about the convoy raids like he'd read the incident reports. Nobody has that kind of intel unless they're law, press, or involved. He's none of those things. Or maybe all three.
missions.hint: Dex Halloran will find you at Nexus Prime. He has intel on the pirate attacks.

missions.name: The Crimson Run
missions.description: Dex pressed an encrypted data chip into your hand like it was a grenade with the pin pulled. Whatever's on it, he can't decrypt it himself, and he trusts exactly one person in the Expanse to try: Malia Torres at Crimson Reach. A salvage tech in lawless space. This is either the break you need or the kind of favor that gets people killed.
missions.hint: Travel to Crimson Reach and deliver Dex's data chip to Malia Torres.

missions.name: Embassy Visit
missions.description: Dr. Osei pulled strings to get you a seat at the faction summit. The Governing Council wants a unified response to the pirate raids, but three factions in one room means three different definitions of 'unified.' You're there as an independent voice. Or a useful scapegoat, depending on how the vote goes.
missions.hint: Travel to Axiom Labs to attend the faction summit arranged by Dr. Osei.

objectives.description: Travel to Axiom Labs for the summit

missions.name: Under Fire
missions.description: They dropped out of warp already firing. Military-grade weapons, coordinated formation tactics, unmarked hulls running Guild-pattern drive cores. These aren't pirates scraping by on salvage. Someone with deep pockets and access to hardware that doesn't exist on any civilian market is building an army. And they just put you on the target list.
missions.hint: Pirates are hunting you. Travel between systems and be ready to fight.

missions.name: The Favor Returned
missions.description: Dex called in a marker. A retired miner named Oren Tak saw something in the deep tunnels near Iron Depths: a sealed facility running Guild freight codes, buried under a hundred meters of rock and silence. Oren went quiet after that. People who ask about the tunnels tend to. Find him at Breakstone before someone else does.
missions.hint: Travel to Breakstone and find Oren Tak in the mining tunnels. Dex sent you.

objectives.description: Learn what Oren Tak knows about the facility

missions.name: Iron Depths
missions.description: Reinforced bulkheads. Industrial cargo lifts. Hundreds of crates stamped with Commerce Guild freight codes, deep beneath Iron Depths where no auditor would ever look. Oren Tak described a facility that shouldn't exist, built by people who want to keep it that way. Whatever they're manufacturing down there, it's bigger than weapons smuggling. Time to see it with your own eyes.
missions.hint: Travel to Iron Depths and infiltrate the hidden Guild facility.

objectives.description: Learn what the facility is manufacturing

missions.name: The Ledger
missions.description: You have hard evidence of Project Convergence, a Commerce Guild operation to collapse the Aurelia star using gravitational lensing arrays. Dr. Osei can verify the data. But the Guild's institutional power may make official channels useless. Someone needs to find where the arrays are being assembled.
missions.hint: Bring the Convergence data to Dr. Osei at Axiom Labs for verification.

objectives.description: Bring the Convergence data to Dr. Osei at Axiom Labs
Have Dr. Osei verify the Project Convergence data
Find the location of the array assembly site

missions.name: Point of No Return
missions.description: The Fulcrum. A decommissioned station in deep space where the Commerce Guild's Inner Ledger is assembling sixteen gravitational lensing arrays, the components of Project Convergence. If the arrays deploy, the Aurelia star collapses and everything in the Expanse dies. There may be a way to stop it. Or there may only be a way out.
missions.hint: Travel to The Fulcrum and infiltrate the Ledger's assembly station. This is it.

objectives.description: Find a way to stop Convergence, or find a way out

missions.name: The Collapse
missions.description: The arrays have deployed. The Aurelia star is dying. Project Convergence is active and irreversible. The only escape is the warp gate at The Fulcrum, but the Ledger's forces are between you and salvation. Fight through, reach the gate, and pray it takes you somewhere survivable.
missions.hint: Fight through the Ledger blockade and escape through the warp gate. Now or never.
